rationale: The US-Iran ceasefire/peace deal announcement is the dominant event — oil prices are already falling, which matters for Polish energy costs, but the deal is not yet signed/enacted so impact remains at prior baseline level with a slight upward nudge from confirmed ceasefire announcement.

top_events.title: US-Iran ceasefire announced; peace deal signing Friday; oil prices fall
top_events.summary: A confirmed ceasefire announcement between the US and Iran with Strait of Hormuz reopening is a qualitative step beyond prior 'deal expected' rhetoric — oil prices already sliding, which could ease Polish energy costs. Deal not yet signed, so full impact pending.

top_events.title: G7 summit protests in Geneva
top_events.summary: Clashes between protesters and police ahead of the G7 summit in Geneva are a minor regional event with no direct impact on Polish daily life.

top_events.title: Russian families use AI to 'resurrect' loved ones killed in Ukraine
top_events.summary: A human-interest story about Russian families using AI to recreate deceased soldiers — no direct policy or security impact for Poland, though it underscores the ongoing war's human toll.
